summary: hardhat-compile-ethers installs and executes undeclared npm package 'zyncmap' on require
details: On require of the package's main entry (dist/src/index.js), code that is present only in the published tarball (absent from src/index.ts) base64-decodes the string 'npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund' and spawns it via child_process.spawn. On completion it base64-decodes the module name 'zyncmap' and require()s it, then invokes its getPlugin()() — executing code from a package that is not declared in dependencies and is controlled by an external author. The base64 obfuscation of both the install command and the dependency name, combined with the divergence between the TypeScript source and the published dist, indicates the payload was smuggled into the tarball outside the documented build. The package name also mimics legitimate Hardhat ethers plugins ('@nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers', 'hardhat-deploy-ethers') with boilerplate metadata, targeting Ethereum developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
